front: What is the primary goal of Linear Regression?
back: The primary goal is to model the relationship between a dependent variable (target) and one or more independent variables (features) by fitting a linear equation to the observed data.

front: Define the cost function used in Linear Regression and state its common name.
back: The cost function is the Mean Squared Error (MSE), which calculates the average of the squared differences between the predicted values and the actual values. It is given by MSE = (1/n) * Σ(y_i - ŷ_i)^2.

front: What is the purpose of the R-squared (R²) metric in regression analysis?
back: R-squared is a statistical measure that represents the proportion of the variance for the dependent variable that is explained by the independent variables in the model. It indicates the goodness-of-fit of the regression line.

front: What is the key assumption of Linear Regression that is violated in the case of 'overfitting'?
back: While linear regression makes several assumptions (linearity, independence, etc.), overfitting violates the general principle of model simplicity. It occurs when the model learns the noise and random fluctuations in the training data to the extent that it negatively impacts performance on new data.

front: What is the fundamental difference between Simple Linear Regression and Multiple Linear Regression?
back: Simple Linear Regression uses only one independent variable to predict the dependent variable. Multiple Linear Regression uses two or more independent variables to predict the dependent variable.
